creator: girgen@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi!

We have lived with the painful threat that a server could possibly die, quite randomly, when stopping a jail. I think we have seen one the backtraces below most of the time.

We recently moved from a home made jail start script using VNET and netgraph, to using the supplied jng script in /usr/share/examples/jails/jng. jng and our old own script use the same strategy, creating two bridges (one for internal private address and one for the external internet facing address), creating two netgraph interfaces and connecting them to the respective bridges. As the jail has started, the ng interfaces are "jailed".

The jng does basically the same thing. We just hoped that it would be more stable and "supported", somehow. :)

We are just about to comission two new servers (using if_bnxt) and now we noticed a different problem:
When either of the jailed interfaces are not connected to an IP address, the kernel will panic when the jail is stopped. This is very much reproducable, it happens determistically.

When add IP-numbers to all jailed interfaces, the jails will stop and start without any panics.

This might relate to this PR or not, I'm not sure?

I have a debug kernel dump, so please advice what you need to find the culprit for this one:

(kgdb) bt
#0  __curthread () at /usr/src/sys/amd64/include/pcpu_aux.h:57
#1  doadump (textdump=<optimized out>) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_shutdown.c:405
